prompt: We need a CTA button for our free trial page. Users land here after reading a blog post. The goal is to get them to start a 14-day trial without requiring a credit card. Current conversion rate is 3% and we want to improve it.

Our internal tool has a feature that lets engineers preview their config changes before deploying. Almost nobody uses it even though it prevents incidents. Write a nudge that appears when someone is about to deploy without previewing.

I need tooltip text for a new 'Export to CSV' button in our analytics dashboard. Users keep asking support how to export data, but the button is right there. The tooltip should gently draw attention without being condescending.

We're launching a new Slack channel for on-call engineers. I need a welcome message that nudges people to set up their notification preferences within the first 24 hours. It should feel helpful, not naggy.

Our SaaS app sends a digest email every Monday. Open rates have dropped to 12%. Rewrite the subject line and preview text to get people to actually open it. The digest contains their team's weekly metrics.
We have an onboarding flow with 7 steps. Most users drop off at step 4 which asks them to invite team members. Write microcopy for a gentle nudge at step 3 that primes them for the invite step.

Our documentation site has a feedback widget. Only 0.5% of visitors use it. I need a non-intrusive prompt that appears after someone spends more than 2 minutes on a page, asking if the docs answered their question.
We're rolling out two-factor authentication as optional. I need notification copy for the settings page that encourages users to enable 2FA without making them feel their account is currently unsafe.

Our mobile app has a rating prompt that fires after 3 sessions. It gets dismissed 90% of the time. Rewrite the prompt text and timing strategy so it feels like a natural moment to leave feedback, not an interruption.

We want users to switch from our legacy API (v1) to the new v2 API. The v1 will be deprecated in 6 months. Write a deprecation notice that nudges migration without creating panic or making users feel punished for being on v1.
